# Implementation Plan: Xray Chart and Insights Enhancements

**Branch**: `007-xray-insights-enhancements` | **Date**: 2025-11-10 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/007-xray-insights-enhancements/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command following the SpecKit workflow.

## Summary

Enhance the Xray treemap visualization with dual color modes (size-based blue gradient and type-based categorical colors), visible node labels with intelligent sizing, and enriched insights with specific file recommendations. Additionally, clean up remaining TypeScript React (.tsx) files from the Vue migration. This feature improves visual analysis capabilities while completing the React-to-Vue migration.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: JavaScript (ES2020+) with Vue 3.5.24 (no TypeScript per project requirement)
**Primary Dependencies**: Vue 3.5+, Pinia 2.3+, ECharts 5.5+, vue-echarts 7.0+, color2k 2.0+
**Storage**: N/A (100% client-side, in-memory state with Pinia)
**Testing**: Vitest with @vue/test-utils and @testing-library/vue
**Target Platform**: Modern browsers (Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+)
**Project Type**: Single-page Vue application (client-side only)
**Performance Goals**: <100ms color calculation, <16ms label rendering (60 FPS), instant mode switching
**Constraints**: WCAG AA contrast compliance (4.5:1), canvas rendering for large trees (10k+ nodes), Web Worker compatibility
**Scale/Scope**: 10,000+ treemap nodes, 50-500MB app binaries, 3+ insight categories

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### Principle I: Client-Side Privacy ✅ PASS
- **Status**: Compliant
- **Verification**: Feature enhances visualization and insights within existing client-side architecture. No new server communication or data transmission introduced. All color calculations and label rendering occur in browser memory.

### Principle II: Performance-First Architecture ✅ PASS
- **Status**: Compliant
- **Verification**: Color mode switching is synchronous (instant) as it only updates ECharts configuration. Label calculation uses existing `node-label-calculator.js` which already handles performance constraints. Insight generation continues to use existing parallel rule execution. No UI thread blocking introduced.

### Principle III: Library-First Design ✅ PASS
- **Status**: Compliant
- **Verification**: Enhancements extend existing libraries (`color-scheme.js`, `node-label-calculator.js`, `insight-engine.js`) with pure functions. New color gradient logic will be added to `color-scheme.js` as standalone function. No React/Vue coupling in business logic.

### Principle IV: Test-Driven Development (TDD) ✅ PASS
- **Status**: Compliant
- **Verification**: Unit tests will be written first for new color gradient function, label visibility logic, and enhanced insight rules using Vitest. Existing fixtures (sample .ipa/.apk files) will be reused for integration tests.

### Principle V: Type Safety & Strict Mode ⚠️ CONDITIONAL PASS
- **Status**: Compliant with project approach
- **Verification**: Project uses JavaScript + JSDoc (not TypeScript strict mode per constitution exception in CLAUDE.md). New functions will include JSDoc type annotations for IDE support and runtime validation.
- **Note**: Constitution specifies TypeScript strict mode, but project has adopted JavaScript-only approach post-Vue migration. This is a documented deviation in project CLAUDE.md.

### Principle VI: Progressive Enhancement ✅ PASS
- **Status**: Compliant
- **Verification**: Feature detection already exists for ECharts. Color mode toggle is optional enhancement - chart renders with default colors if mode selection fails. Labels gracefully degrade (hidden on small nodes) per existing logic.

### Principle VII: Specification-First Workflow ✅ PASS
- **Status**: Compliant
- **Verification**: Following SpecKit workflow: spec.md created → plan.md in progress → tasks.md next → implementation follows. User stories prioritized (P1: color modes, P2: labels, P3: insights).

### Summary
**Overall Status**: ✅ PASS WITH DEVIATION NOTED

All principles satisfied. **One documented deviation**: Constitution mandates TypeScript strict mode (Principle V), but project uses JavaScript + JSDoc approach. This deviation is approved in project CLAUDE.md and aligns with 006-vue-migration decision to eliminate TypeScript.

**Pre-Phase 0 Gate**: CLEARED - Proceed to research phase.

**Structure Decision**: Single-page Vue application (client-side only). All modifications target existing Vue components and library functions. Migration cleanup involves deleting 21 .tsx files (components + tests) that have been superseded by .vue equivalents.
